rent.note: furnished 1+1 in Kadikoy/Sisli/Beyoglu; 30,000-55,000 TL ($900-1,650); mid-point nomad area

utilities.note: electric + water + internet; utilities $50-100; internet $10-15 separately

groceries.note: Migros/BIM + local pazars; groceries $150-250/mo range; inflation elevated

diningOut.note: simit $0.30, lokantas $4-8, quality mezes; eating out very affordable vs Western cities

transit.note: Istanbul Card monthly pass ~$40; excellent metro/tram/ferry network

coworking.note: Impact Hub, IDEA Kadikoy; solid coworking scene; $100-200/mo range

simData.note: Turkcell/Vodafone TR tourist SIMs; resident plan competitive

misc.note: hammam, museums, Bosphorus ferries, gym, entertainment; rich cultural scene

accomSeasonality._doc: 12 monthly multipliers applied to RENT ONLY (Jan..Dec). 1.0 = base. Raise for peak tourist/weather months, lower for off-peak.

evidence.quote: A 1+1 or small 2+1 apartment typically costs between 30,000 and 55,000 TL per month (approximately 900–1,650 USD). One-bedroom apartments in the city center rent for $900/month

A digital nomad should plan for approximately $1,366/month in Istanbul. the average monthly budget for a single person seeking a comfortable but not luxurious lifestyle should be approximately $1,500 to $2,500 USD

evidence.claim: Istanbul groceries $150-250; monthly public transport ~$40; utilities $50-100
evidence.quote: Groceries cost $150–$250 and a monthly public transport pass costs around $40. Utilities cost between $50–$100 monthly for an average apartment, with internet adding another $10–$15

evidence.claim: Many live in Istanbul on $1,000/month by renting outside center and cooking at home
evidence.quote: Many people live in Istanbul on $1,000/month by renting outside the city center, using public transport, and managing dining expenses
